title: Piranmalai Kodunkundreeswar Temple
tagline: Piranmalai Kodunkundreeswar Temple — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Piranmalai Kodunkundreeswar Temple is a Hindu temple situated at Piranmalai, a hilly village located in Singampunari Taluk in Sivagangai District, Tamil Nadu State, India.
overview.description: Piranmalai Kodunkundreeswar Temple is a Hindu temple situated at Piranmalai, a hilly village located in Singampunari Taluk in Sivagangai District, Tamil Nadu State, India. Kodunkundreeswar and Bhairavar temples are located in this village. Piranmalai is a fortified hill at an elevation of over 2,000 feet. This is a last outcrop of the Eastern Ghats, with a Bhairavar temple and five peedas in the rocks of the hill called the Pandava Therthams, is also sacred to the Muslims with a dargah of Waliullah Sheikh Abdullah Shaheb on its peak.

topPlaces.description: Singampunari is a Taluk in Sivagangai district, in Tamil Nadu, India. The town is located at a distance of 44 km (27 mi) from Karaikudi.

topPlaces.name: Natham
topPlaces.description: Natham (Tamil: [nattam]) is a town in Dindigul district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Vairavanpatti
topPlaces.description: Vairavanpatti is a village located on the way from Madurai and Karaikudi in Sivaganga district of Tamil Nadu, India. This village derives its name from the temple dedicated to Lord Kala Bhairav who is known in Tamil language as Vairavan.

topPlaces.name: Ammankovilpathi
topPlaces.description: Ammankovilpathi is a village located 15 km from Mecheri in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. This village has a population of around 2000.

topPlaces.name: Sadurvedamangalam
topPlaces.description: Sadur Veda Mangalam is a village in Singampunari Taluk, Sivaganga district, Tamil Nadu, India, 400 kilometers from the state capital of Chennai.

topPlaces.name: Puluthipatti
topPlaces.description: Puluthipatti village gram Panchayat is located in the S. Pudur (Semmampatti Pudur) Panchayat Union of Sivaganga district, Tamil Nadu. This panchayat falls under the Tiruppattur Assembly Constituency and the Sivaganga Lok Sabha Constituency.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Piranmalai Kodunkundreeswar Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Piranmalai Kodunkundreeswar Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Piranmalai Kodunkundreeswar Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Madurai Airport (59 km) and the nearest railway station is Madurai Junction (50 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Piranmalai Kodunkundreeswar Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Piranmalai Kodunkundreeswar Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Piranmalai Kodunkundreeswar Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Piranmalai Kodunkundreeswar Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Piranmalai Kodunkundreeswar Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Piranmalai, Singampunari block, Singampunari, Natham, Vairavanpatti.
